column6: Your log template + the most common Night 1 mistake.
column7:
How was Night 1?

The most common Night 1 mistake: expectation management.

Night 1 is NOT about having a lucid dream. It is about calibrating your WBTB timing and beginning the body-scan practice. If you set an alarm, woke up, stayed awake for 20-30 minutes, and returned to sleep with relaxed awareness — Night 1 was a success.

The log template is in Module 1. Use it. The data you collect in the first 7 nights is the foundation for everything that follows.

Any questions — post in #protocol-qa in Discord.

column5: One week in. Here is your personalised next step.
column6: Based on where you are in the protocol, here is what to focus on now.
column7:
One week in the course.

Here is your decision tree for next steps:

Had 0 lucid dreams: Focus exclusively on WBTB timing and body-scan quality. Do not add any technique until you can consistently cross the hypnagogic threshold without snapping back awake. Module 3 covers this.

Had 1-2 lucid dreams: You are on track. Start Module 4 stabilisation work.

Had 3+ lucid dreams: Jump to Module 5. You are ready for intentional dream work.

Whatever your path: the course has you covered. And the community has people exactly where you are.

Keep going.

column5: The DEILD technique: everything you need to start tonight
column6: Re-enter dreams within 30 seconds of waking. Step by step.
column7:
DEILD — Dream-Exit Induced Lucid Dreaming — is the technique most likely to produce your highest-frequency lucid dreaming period.

The mechanism: when you naturally wake from a REM period, your brain is primed. Acetylcholine is elevated. Dream content is still present in working memory. If you remain absolutely still and keep your eyes closed, you can re-enter the dream with full awareness within 30-60 seconds.

The key variables:
• No movement whatsoever upon waking. Movement signals to the brain that you are awake and need to be alert.
• Eyes closed before conscious awareness fully returns.
• Hold a neutral, receptive mental state. Do not try to visualise — just observe what comes.

Full technique breakdown with timing specifics is in Module 1.

Try it on your next natural morning wake. Post in #advanced-techniques when you get a result.
